market_summary: This week extended the risk-off regime as equities absorbed higher oil prices, rising yields, and reduced odds of policy easing. By Friday, the S&P 500 closed at 6,506.48 after a 1.9% weekly drop and now sits 5.0% lower year to date, while the Nasdaq is down 6.9% year to date. The Federal Reserve held rates at 3.50-3.75% on March 18 and emphasized elevated uncertainty. Cross-asset conditions stayed defensive, with crude elevated and rates rising, keeping pressure on long-duration equity exposure.

momentum.notes: Long momentum likely struggled in headline-driven swings; downside momentum required fast execution.

mean_reversion.notes: Likely viable intraday after volatility spikes, but only with strict risk controls.

trend.notes: Short-biased trend setups probably worked better than long continuation setups this week.

volume_breakout.notes: Breakout quality likely degraded as macro headlines increased false follow-through.

support_resistance.notes: Key levels likely provided better structure than pure breakout entries in this tape.

vwap.notes: VWAP-based intraday discipline likely helped avoid overtrading in volatile sessions.

relative_strength.notes: Sector-relative trades (energy/defensives vs growth) were likely among the cleaner opportunities.

news_catalyst.notes: News catalysts were abundant but required smaller size due to reversal risk.

new_rules: Do not run full-size long breakout trades while VIX remains in the mid-20s.
Use rates and oil as hard risk filters before adding index beta.
Prioritize relative-strength rotation over broad-market direction calls.
Cut gross exposure before weekend headline risk when conflict escalation is unresolved.
Treat any relief rally as tactical until volatility and breadth both improve.
regime_forecast: Next week starts risk-off unless we see simultaneous cooling in oil and yields. Baseline expectation is continued choppy downside pressure with intermittent sharp relief rallies around geopolitical headlines and policy repricing.
focus_areas: Monitor VIX behavior versus recent highs for regime transition clues
Track 10-year yield sensitivity of high-multiple technology names
Watch energy leadership for exhaustion or continuation
Prepare scenario plans for the April 28-29 FOMC meeting path
